preamble: The shift from the lateral approximant [l] to the voiceless labiodental fricative [f] is an extreme form of labialization and cluster merger. It typically occurs in word-final clusters where a vocalized lateral merges with an existing labial sound, or through developmental substitution in child language.

examples.note: The vocalized 'dark L' merges with the following fricative, resulting in a single labiodental sound.
Devoicing of the labial element in words where the lateral is heavily vocalized.
